groups.label: Backend Systems & Architecture
groups.description: The strongest backend-facing work: service architecture, APIs, concurrency, and integration-heavy systems.

groups.label: Applied Data & Intelligent Systems
groups.description: Projects where data processing, AI workflows, or prediction logic make the system meaningfully more interesting.

items.title: Pantheon
items.summary: A unified game-library backend designed as a modular monolith with explicit architecture boundaries and real-time event delivery.
items.impact: Shows modern backend architecture thinking through hexagonal design, SSE updates, migration tooling, architecture tests, and enforced quality gates.
notes: This is the strongest public architecture-forward project in the portfolio right now.
The backend README provides unusually strong proof: package-by-feature design, ports and adapters, quality gates, and continuous integration discipline.

items.title: Stock Portfolio Management
items.summary: A secure REST backend for portfolio tracking with authentication, persistence, and live market data integration.
items.impact: Strong conventional backend signal through Spring Security, JWT authentication, MySQL persistence, OpenAPI docs, and external finance API integration.
notes: This should remain one of the primary flagship projects because it reads immediately as serious backend/API work.
It pairs well with Pantheon by showing a more traditional service stack and product domain.

items.title: Image WebCrawler
items.summary: A multi-threaded crawler that recursively explores in-domain pages and extracts images while controlling duplication and resource usage.
items.impact: Distinctive systems work that demonstrates concurrency, recursive job creation, URL validation, thread-safe data structures, and test coverage.
notes: This is the most technically distinctive backend project in the set and adds memorability without leaving the systems story.
The README gives enough implementation detail to support deeper writeups later.

items.title: Uni-link
items.summary: A backend and integration layer for a unified social-media management workflow across multiple platforms.
items.impact: Useful as a breadth project because it shows FastAPI, third-party platform integrations, and product-facing utility outside the Java stack.
notes: Public repo documentation is thinner than the top three backend projects, so it should sit below them in visual priority.
Still worth keeping because it adds stack breadth and real integration flavor.

items.title: Stonks
items.summary: A stock prediction application that combines social-media sentiment with historical market data to estimate future movement.
items.impact: Adds technical range through Django, NLP, finance APIs, and a more experimental decisioning pipeline without abandoning the backend story.
notes: This is the best range project to keep because it is distinct, specific, and still technically layered.
It should be framed as applied backend/data experimentation rather than an ML vanity project.

items.title: Telegram GPT Bot
items.summary: A multilingual Telegram bot that uses text and voice workflows to make AI assistance more accessible in lower-access settings.
items.impact: Memorable and human-centered, with a strong conversation angle around applied AI, speech, and messaging interfaces layered onto a real backend workflow.
notes: This stays because it adds personality and a more modern system flavor, even though the public repo docs are currently thin.
Portfolio copy should carry the context here more than the repo currently does.

items.title: Insta-Diagnosis
items.summary: A web application for disease prediction using medical scans, secure record handling, and image pre-processing before model inference.
items.impact: Rounds out the applied-data category with healthcare, computer vision, and secure record-management context.
notes: This is a secondary project, not a lead project, because the public proof is thinner than the top backend entries.
It still earns a slot because it gives the applied-data category a broader shape than finance alone.
